verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone, but during stress, viral infection, and malignant transformation it is well-documented to traffic to the cell surface where it is exposed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Surface GRP78 has been exploited as a tumor-specific target by antibodies, ADCs, and peptide-drug conjugates in multiple cancer programs. The surface form retains its own stable membrane association in stressed/tumor cells, qualifying as cell-state-induced dual localization rather than transient recruitment.
